title: Kusuma Bhai vs Govindappa S/O. Ramappa Chitragar on 27 February, 2017
body: Kusuma Bhai vs Govindappa S/O. Ramappa Chitragar on 27 February, 2017 Author: B.Sreenivase Gowda Bench: B.Sreenivase Gowda 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT DHARWAD BENCH DATED THIS THE 27TH DAY OF FEBRUARY, 2017 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE B.SREENIVASE GOWDA REVIEW PETITION NO.100024/2014 IN WRIT PETITION NO.84861/2013(GM- CPC ) BETWEEN: 1. SMT. KUSUMA BHAI, W/O. SIDRAMAPPA CHITRAGAR, AGE: 62 YEARS, OCC: HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O.KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 2. SMT. RUKMINI, W/O. RAMESH CHITRAGAR, AGE: 43 YEARS, OCC: HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O. KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 3. KUMAR CHETAN, S/O. RAMESH CHITRAGAR, AGE: 19 YEARS, 4. KUMARI PALLAVI, D/O. RAMESH CHITRAGAR, 2 AGE: 17 YEARS, PETITIONER NO.4 IS MINOR REPRESENTED BY THEIR NATURAL MOTHER PETITIONER NO.2, SMT. RUKINI, W/O. RAMESH CHITRAGAR. 5. SMT. RADHA, W/O. IRANNA CHITRAGAR, AGE: 48 YEARS, OCC: HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O. DAM ROAD, NEAR OLD BUS STAND, HOSPETE, NOW AT KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 6. SMT. TULASA, W/O. RAVI DANGE, AGE: 45 YEARS, OCC: ANGANWADI TEACHER, R/O. KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 7. SMT. SHOBHA, W/O. MAHADEVAPPA CHAVAN, AGE: 42 YEARS, OCC: HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O. BASAVANA BAGEWADI, DIST: BIJAPUR, NOW AT KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 8. RAMANNA, S/O. SHIDRAMAPPA CHITRAGAR, AGE: 35 YEARS, OCC: AGRICULTURE & BUSINESS, R/O. KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. ... PETITIONERS (By SRI. S. C. BHUTI, ADV.) 3 AND: 1. GOVINDAPPA, S/O. RAMAPPA CHITRAGAR, AGE: 69 YEARS, OCC: CARPENTER, R/O. KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. 2. GANAPATHI, S/O. RAMAPPA CHITRAGAR, AGE: 64 YEARS, OCC: CARPENTER, R/O.KUNNUR, TQ: SHIGGAON, DIST: HAVERI. ... RESPONDENTS (By SRI. SANTOSH B. MANE, ADV. FOR R.1 & R.2) THIS REVIEW PETITION IS FILED UNDER ORDER 47 RULE 1 R/W SECTION 114 OF CPC , PRAYING TO REVIEW THE ORDER DATED 20.01.2014 PASSED IN THE WP NO.84861/2013 ON THE FILE OF THE HON'BLE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA, DHARWAD BENCH. THIS REVIEW PETITION COMING ON FOR FINAL HEARING, THIIS DAY, THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING: ORDER Petitioners have preferred this Review Petition seeking review of the order dated 20-01-2014 passed in WP No.84861/2013. 4 2. Perused the order of this Court dated 20-01-2014 passed in WP No.84861/2013. 3. Sri. Bhuti, the learned Counsel for the petitioners submits this Court under the impression that the father of petitioners in his written statement filed in O. S.41 /2008 has admitted the joint right of the plaintiffs and defendants over enjoying the fruits of mango trees standing in the suit land has dismissed the writ petition of the petitioners. Therefore, there is error apparent in the judgment of this Court. Learned Counsel for the petitioners further submits, the first appellate Court allowed I.A.No.23 filed by the defendants and set aside the order of temporary injunction granted by the trial Court in favour of the plaintiffs on the ground that plaintiffs have failed to make out a prima facie case for granting an order of 5 temporary injunction in their favour and not on the ground that father of the plaintiffs while filing written statement in O.S.No.41/2008 have admitted the joint rights of plaintiffs and defendants over enjoying the fruits of mango trees standing in the suit land. Hence, he prays for allowing the petition and setting aside the order passed in the writ petition and to hear the writ petition afresh. 4. Sri. Santhosh B.Mane, learned Counsel appearing for the respondents submits, suit O. S.78 /2008 filed by the petitioners against the respondents for the relief of permanent injunction, restraining the respondents from interfering with the alleged possession and enjoyment of the plaintiffs over the suit property itself came to be dismissed and therefore, review petition does not survive for consideration and it may be dismissed accordingly. 6 5. Petitioners filed a suit in O. S.78 /2008 against the respondents seeking the relief of permanent injunction restraining the respondents from interfering with the possession and enjoyment of the plaintiffs over the suit schedule land. In the said suit, petitioners made an application under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 of CPC seeking an order of temporary injunction against the respondents, restraining them from interfering with the possession and enjoyment of the plaintiffs/petitioners over the suit land during the pendency of the suit. The trial Court after hearing the learned Counsel appearing for the parties, allowed the application and granted an order of temporary injunction restraining the respondents from interfering with the possession and enjoyment of the plaintiffs over the suit land. Defendants aggrieved by the said order of the trial Court challenged the same by preferring MA 23/2011 7 before the first appellate Court. The first appellate Court allowed the miscellaneous appeal and set aside the order passed by the trial Court on the ground that petitioners have failed to make out a prima facie case for granting an order of temporary injunction in their favour. Ultimately, suit O. S.78 /2008 filed by the petitioners against the respondents itself came to be dismissed. However, perusal of the written statement filed in O. S.41 /2008 by the father of the petitioners would show, petitioners father has not admitted the joint right of the plaintiffs (petitioners herein) and defendants (respondents herein) over enjoying the fruits of the mango trees standing in the suit land. Perusal of the plaint of O. S.78 /2008 filed by the petitioners against the respondents would show, plaintiffs/petitioners have averred that the suit land is in joint possession and enjoyment of the parties. In view of the above, it is just 8 and proper to modify the observation made in para 5 of the order commencing from "In the said suit (O. S.41 /2008), the present plaintiffs' father filed written statement admitting the joint right of plaintiffs and defendants over enjoying the fruits of mango trees standing in the suit land. The lower appellate Court, considering this material aspect of the matter is justified in allowing MA 23/2011 filed by the defendants and setting aside the order of temporary injunction passed in I.A.I by the trial Court in O.S.78/2008 and rejecting I.A.I" as under : "In the suit O. S.78 /2008 filed by the petitioners against the respondents for the relief of permanent injunction, petitioners have admitted that the suit land is in joint possession and enjoyment of the parties to the suit. The lower appellate Court considering this aspect of the matter and holding that plaintiffs have failed to prove prima facie case for grant of an order of injunction, was justified in allowing MA 23/2011 filed by the defendants (respondent herein) and setting aside the order of temporary injunction granted by the trial Court in O.S.78/2012 and rejecting I.A.No.I" 9 Accordingly, the Review petition stands disposed of. However, the operative portion of the order dated 20.01.2014 passed in WP No.84861/2013 remains undisturbed. Sd/- JUDGE Mgn/-
